examples.title: Simple Thread Scheduling Simulation
examples.description: Demonstrates basic thread creation and scheduling in Python using threading module
examples.code:
import threading
import time

# Define thread function
def thread_task(thread_name, duration):
    print(f"Thread {thread_name} STARTED")
    time.sleep(duration)  # Simulates work
    print(f"Thread {thread_name} COMPLETED")

# Create threads
threads = []
for i in range(3):
    t = threading.Thread(target=thread_task, args=(f"T{i+1}", 2))
    threads.append(t)

# Start all threads
for t in threads:
    t.start()

# Wait for completion
for t in threads:
    t.join()

print("All threads finished")
examples.explanation: Creates 3 threads that run concurrently. The OS scheduler decides actual execution order. Thread.join() ensures main thread waits for all to complete.
examples.timeComplexity: Depends on OS scheduler and thread count
examples.spaceComplexity: O(n) for n threads (stack space per thread)

examples.title: Priority-Based Thread Scheduling
examples.description: Demonstrates priority scheduling where higher priority threads execute first
examples.explanation: Creates threads with different priorities. In actual OS, scheduler would give CPU to highest priority thread first. POSIX scheduling attributes can be set for real priority.
examples.timeComplexity: O(n log n) for n threads when sorting by priority

examples.title: Round-Robin Scheduling Simulation
examples.description: Simulates Round-Robin scheduling with time quantum
examples.explanation: Simulates Round-Robin where each thread gets TIME_QUANTUM (4 units). If burst remains, thread goes to back of queue. Shows fair scheduling with quantum expiration handling.

examples.title: Multilevel Queue Scheduling Algorithm
examples.description: Pseudocode showing multilevel queue scheduling with different algorithms per queue
examples.code:
ALGORITHM MultilevelQueueScheduling
BEGIN
  // Define queues with priorities (0 = highest)
  CREATE queue[0] = SYSTEM_QUEUE  // System threads
  CREATE queue[1] = INTERACTIVE_QUEUE  // Interactive threads
  CREATE queue[2] = BATCH_QUEUE  // Batch/background threads
  
  // Set scheduling algorithm for each queue
  queue[0].algorithm = PRIORITY  // System uses priority
  queue[1].algorithm = ROUND_ROBIN  // Interactive uses RR
  queue[2].algorithm = FCFS  // Batch uses FCFS
  
  PROCEDURE schedule()
    FOR i = 0 TO 2 DO
      IF queue[i] is NOT empty THEN
        // Only schedule from highest non-empty queue
        SELECT thread FROM queue[i] USING queue[i].algorithm
        EXECUTE thread ON CPU
        RETURN
      END IF
    END FOR
  END PROCEDURE
  
  PROCEDURE addThread(thread)
    // Add thread to appropriate queue based on type
    IF thread.type = SYSTEM THEN
      queue[0].enqueue(thread)
    ELSE IF thread.type = INTERACTIVE THEN
      queue[1].enqueue(thread)
    ELSE
      queue[2].enqueue(thread)
    END IF
  END PROCEDURE
END
examples.explanation: Multilevel queue separates threads by type, each with its own scheduling algorithm. Higher priority queues are served first, which can cause starvation of lower queues - may need time slicing between queues.
examples.timeComplexity: O(1) to select queue, depends on queue algorithm for thread selection
examples.spaceComplexity: O(n) for storing threads across all queues
